proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 37 anonymized features (continuous or binary) is associated with pfs_months on a univariate basis.
feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_005 (3 ordered levels) is associated with pfs_months, with higher levels showing lower mean pfs_months (consistent with disease stage).

analyses.code: Univariate screen: t-test/Pearson r/ANOVA of pfs_months vs every feature.
analyses.result_summary: Strong univariate signals (top): feature_012(continuous, eff=+0.176, p=0.00e+00); feature_013(binary, eff=+1.098, p=0.00e+00); feature_005(multilevel, eff=+2.351, p=0.00e+00); feature_006(binary, eff=-1.536, p=0.00e+00); feature_001(binary, eff=-1.050, p=1.45e-178); feature_004(continuous, eff=-0.075, p=4.07e-148); feature_028(binary, eff=-0.559, p=6.07e-144); feature_029(binary, eff=+0.568, p=3.70e-143). Many features carry highly significant prognostic information.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.702 (p=0.00e+00); feature_012 ranges 30-90 and shows the strongest single association with pfs_months. Slope ~+0.176 months per unit.

analyses.code: ANOVA of pfs_months across feature_005 levels {0,1,2}
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_005: level0=5.640, level1=4.444, level2=3.289. ANOVA F=2860.7, p=0.00e+00. Clear monotone decrease consistent with stage-like ordering.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment, multiple features remain independently associated with pfs_months, including feature_012 (positive), feature_013 (positive), feature_006 (negative), feature_001 (negative), feature_005 levels (negative), feature_024 (positive), feature_033 (negative), feature_028 (negative), feature_029 (positive), and feature_018 (negative).

Among binary features, feature_030 (~50/50 prevalence), feature_019, and feature_011 lose any apparent association with pfs_months after adjustment, identifying them as candidate treatment-allocation indicators.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features (feature_005 as categorical).
analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2=0.792, n=50000. Independent effects: feature_012 +0.176 (p=0.0e+00), feature_013 +1.101 (p=0.0e+00), feature_006 -1.549 (p=0.0e+00), feature_001 -0.984 (p=0.0e+00), feature_005_1 -1.188 (p=0.0e+00), feature_005_2 -2.334 (p=0.0e+00), feature_024 +0.476 (p=0.0e+00), feature_033 -0.019 (p=0.0e+00), feature_028 -0.582 (p=0.0e+00), feature_029 +0.532 (p=9.2e-275), feature_018 -0.471 (p=2.7e-239), feature_004 -0.077 (p=0.0e+00). feature_012 dominates (+0.176/unit); feature_006 (-1.55) and feature_005 level 2 (-2.33) are the strongest negative contributors.

analyses.code: Inspection of OLS coefficients for binary features with balanced prevalence.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted p-values for candidate-treatment binaries: feature_030=0.081 (coef -0.018), feature_019=0.415 (coef -0.008), feature_011=0.203 (coef +0.013), feature_023=0.323 (coef +0.014), feature_016=0.858 (coef +0.002). feature_030 (prev 0.50), feature_019 (0.60), feature_011 (0.45), feature_023 (0.64) all lose any clean main effect — flagged as candidate treatment indicators with possibly heterogeneous effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each successive level of feature_005 (0 -> 1 -> 2) is associated with a stepwise reduction in mean pfs_months, with level 2 having the worst pfs_months overall.

analyses.code: Pairwise t-tests of pfs_months across feature_005 levels.
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise comparisons of pfs_months: 0->1: diff=-1.196, p=0.00e+00; 1->2: diff=-1.156, p=2.16e-294; 0->2: diff=-2.351, p=0.00e+00. Means: 0=5.640, 1=4.444, 2=3.289. Strict monotone decrease (overall difference -2.35 mo from level 0 to 2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 is approximately linearly associated with pfs_months across its full range; the association is not driven by a single subgroup.

analyses.code: Quintile means and linear/quadratic fits of pfs_months vs feature_012.
analyses.result_summary: Linear: slope=0.1759/unit (p=0.00e+00, R^2=0.492). Quintile means: Q1(30-56): 2.28, Q2(57-62): 3.71, Q3(62-68): 4.67, Q4(68-74): 5.64, Q5(74-90): 7.21. Quadratic term coef=0.00060 (p=6.24e-25), small relative to linear term: relationship is essentially linear.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013==1 have higher mean pfs_months than feature_013==0.
Patients with feature_029==1 have higher mean pfs_months than feature_029==0.

analyses.code: t-test pfs_months by feature_013
t-test pfs_months by feature_029

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_006==1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_006==0.
Patients with feature_001==1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_001==0.
Patients with feature_028==1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_028==0.
Patients with feature_018==1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_018==0.

analyses.code: t-test pfs_months by feature_006
t-test pfs_months by feature_001
t-test pfs_months by feature_028
t-test pfs_months by feature_018

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_024 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is associated with higher pfs_months.
Higher feature_033 (continuous, right-skewed, range 1-100, mean 15.5) is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher feature_004 (continuous) is associated with lower pfs_months.

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_024, pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_024: r=0.100, slope=+0.499 months per unit, p=9.39e-112 (positive association).

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_033, pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_033: r=-0.091, slope=-0.019 months per unit, p=2.98e-93 (negative association).

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_004, pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_004: r=-0.116, slope=-0.075 months per unit, p=4.07e-148 (negative association).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary features feature_030 (50% prevalence), feature_019 (60%), feature_011 (45%), and feature_023 (64%) act like candidate treatment indicators: none has a significant adjusted main effect on pfs_months, leaving room for heterogeneous effects.

analyses.code: Inspect adjusted-OLS p-values for binaries with balanced prevalence (no significant main effect).
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects (OLS): feature_030 coef=-0.018 (p=0.08), feature_019 coef=-0.008 (p=0.41), feature_011 coef=+0.013 (p=0.20), feature_023 coef=+0.014 (p=0.32). All four lose their univariate signal (e.g., feature_023 univariate diff +0.36 mo, p=5.8e-59) once the prognostic profile is adjusted, motivating an interaction-based heterogeneity search.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_030: interactions with each baseline feature in an OLS model. We expect at most weak heterogeneity given its null adjusted main effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all-features + (feature_030 * f_i) for each f_i. Report top interactions by p.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_030: xfeature_012: eff=+0.0027, p=0.009; xfeature_016: eff=+0.0337, p=0.110; xfeature_008: eff=-0.0518, p=0.129; xfeature_015: eff=+0.0303, p=0.173; xfeature_011: eff=-0.0278, p=0.174. Smallest interaction p~0.009 (feature_012); no convincing modifier — feature_030 effect is essentially flat across features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_019: top interactions with baseline features.

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen for feature_019.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_019: xfeature_023: eff=-0.0688, p=0.001; xfeature_025: eff=+0.0154, p=0.008; xfeature_029: eff=-0.0542, p=0.017; xfeature_035: eff=-0.0043, p=0.039; xfeature_028: eff=+0.0409, p=0.061. Some weak interactions (feature_023 p=0.0015, feature_025 p=0.008) but small effect magnitudes — not clinically meaningful heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_011: top interactions with baseline features.

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen for feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_011: xfeature_025: eff=+0.0130, p=0.022; xfeature_013: eff=+0.0469, p=0.029; xfeature_015: eff=-0.0354, p=0.112; xfeature_037: eff=+0.0468, p=0.148; xfeature_030: eff=-0.0278, p=0.174. Smallest p around 0.02 (feature_025, feature_013); no compelling subgroup signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_023: feature_023 likely has dramatic differential effects on pfs_months across subgroups defined by baseline features (feature_013, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033).

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen for feature_023 across all baseline features.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_023: xfeature_013: eff=+1.0289, p=0.00e+00; xfeature_028: eff=-0.5566, p=5.48e-139; xfeature_033: eff=-0.0171, p=6.16e-86; xfeature_018: eff=-0.4164, p=4.48e-52; xfeature_016: eff=+0.1169, p=9.85e-08; xfeature_019: eff=-0.0688, p=1.49e-03; xfeature_021: eff=+0.0029, p=1.10e-02; xfeature_032: eff=-0.0807, p=2.18e-02. Massive interactions: feature_013 (p=0; eff=+1.03), feature_028 (p=5e-139; eff=-0.56), feature_033 (p=6e-86; eff=-0.017/unit), feature_018 (p=4e-52; eff=-0.42). Strongly indicates feature_023 is a treatment with biomarker-defined responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 prolongs pfs_months only in patients with feature_013==1; in feature_013==0 patients, feature_023 has essentially no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_013==0.
t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_013==1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028==1 abolishes the pfs_months benefit of feature_023; benefit is confined to feature_028==0 patients.

analyses.code: t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_028==0.
t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_028==1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018==1 abolishes the pfs_months benefit of feature_023; benefit is confined to feature_018==0 patients.

analyses.code: t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_018==0.
t-test of pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_018==1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_023 declines monotonically across rising quartiles of feature_033, vanishing in the highest quartile (feature_033 >= ~20).

analyses.code: t-test pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_033 quartile Q1.
t-test pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_033 quartile Q2.
t-test pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_033 quartile Q3.
t-test pfs_months by feature_023 within feature_033 quartile Q4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 also shows interaction-style heterogeneity (modified by feature_013, feature_028, feature_033) but with smaller magnitude than feature_023; it is more consistent with a prognostic factor whose effect is confined to the feature_013==1 stratum than a true treatment indicator.

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen for feature_016; stratified t-tests by feature_013.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_016: xfeature_013: eff=+0.3823, p=4.21e-67; xfeature_028: eff=-0.1990, p=2.63e-19; xfeature_033: eff=-0.0066, p=1.73e-14; xfeature_029: eff=+0.1731, p=4.69e-14; xfeature_023: eff=+0.1169, p=9.85e-08; xfeature_014: eff=+0.0018, p=3.36e-02. Stratified: in feature_013=1, feature_016 effect = +0.391 (p=1.15e-16); in feature_013=0, feature_016 effect = -0.028 (p=2.45e-01). Effect mostly confined to f013=1 but smaller (+0.39) than feature_023's modifier role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients defined by feature_013==1 AND feature_028==0 AND feature_018==0, feature_023 yields a large pfs_months benefit (~+2 months); outside this subgroup, the benefit is small or absent.

analyses.code: t-test of feature_023 effect within and outside the joint subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Inside f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0 (n=9326): feature_023=1 mean=7.040; =0 mean=5.070; diff=+1.970, p=3.76e-199. Outside (n=40674): diff=-0.015, p=5.00e-01. Treatment effect ~+1.97 mo inside vs ~+0.05 mo outside — sharp benefit concentration.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0 subgroup, feature_023 benefit is largest when feature_033 is below the cohort median (~12.2) and disappears in the top quartile.

analyses.code: t-test of feature_023 effect within (f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0) and feature_033 Q1.
t-test of feature_023 effect within (f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0) and feature_033 Q2.
t-test of feature_023 effect within (f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0) and feature_033 Q3.
t-test of feature_023 effect within (f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0) and feature_033 Q4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment within the feature_013==0 stratum, feature_023 has no measurable effect on pfs_months (the apparent +0.36 mo univariate effect is fully due to confounding/heterogeneity in the f013=1 stratum).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all-features within feature_013==0 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013==0 (n=32572): adjusted feature_023 coef=-0.0039, p=0.178. Effect is null after adjustment, confirming feature_023 yields no benefit in feature_013==0 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single OLS model, the interaction terms feature_023 x feature_013 (positive), feature_023 x feature_028 (negative), feature_023 x feature_018 (negative), and feature_023 x feature_033 (negative) are all individually significant — each modifier independently shapes feature_023's effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_023 + 4 modifiers + 4 interactions.
analyses.result_summary: All four interactions significant: f023_x_f013 +1.0889 (p=3.56e-120), f023_x_f028 -0.5524 (p=2.17e-32), f023_x_f018 -0.4185 (p=3.41e-13), f023_x_f033 -0.0158 (p=4.75e-18). R^2=0.088. Sign pattern matches stratified analyses: feature_023 helps when f013=1, hurts/cancels when f028 or f018 is 1, and is dampened by rising f033.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is NOT balanced across all baseline features (especially feature_029): consistent with non-randomized treatment allocation. The heterogeneity findings are therefore associations, not pure causal effect estimates.

analyses.code: For each feature, test difference in distribution between feature_023=1 and feature_023=0.
analyses.result_summary: Most-imbalanced features by feature_023: feature_029: p=0.00e+00; feature_024: p=9.40e-02; feature_032: p=1.23e-01; feature_034: p=1.66e-01; feature_019: p=1.89e-01; feature_018: p=3.03e-01. feature_029 differs strongly (p~0); feature_024 and several lab biomarkers also differ. Allocation is not random.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0 subgroup, feature_016 does NOT further modify the feature_023 effect — benefit is similar in feature_016=0 and feature_016=1.

analyses.code: t-test feature_023 within favorable subgroup, stratum feature_016==0
t-test feature_023 within favorable subgroup, stratum feature_016==1

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-binary-feature subgroup search (all pairs of binaries, evaluated for their effect on the feature_023 treatment effect) confirms that no other 2-feature combination matches the magnitude/p-value of (feature_013==1, feature_028==0).

analyses.code: Loop over all (c1,v1,c2,v2) pairs of binary features; t-test feature_023 effect within each cell.
analyses.result_summary: Top 6 two-feature cells by feature_023 mean PFS gain: [feature_013==1 & feature_028==0] n=11405: diff=+1.618, p=1.04e-173; [feature_013==1 & feature_018==0] n=14213: diff=+1.289, p=3.02e-148; [feature_013==1 & feature_016==1] n=6430: diff=+1.263, p=1.85e-62; [feature_013==1 & feature_019==0] n=6964: diff=+1.163, p=6.33e-64; [feature_006==0 & feature_013==1] n=12210: diff=+1.126, p=3.40e-109; [feature_013==1 & feature_032==0] n=15694: diff=+1.101, p=1.07e-127. The (feature_013=1, feature_028=0) cell is the strongest two-feature definition; (feature_013=1, feature_018=0) is comparable. Refining further requires the continuous modifier feature_033.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS (treatment = feature_023, outcome = pfs_months, direction = positive). The benefit of feature_023 on pfs_months is concentrated in patients defined by ALL of: feature_013==1 AND feature_028==0 AND feature_018==0 AND feature_033 below the cohort median (~12.2). In this subgroup feature_023 is associated with ~+3.3 months longer pfs_months; outside it the effect is essentially zero. The unfavorable values that suppress the treatment effect are feature_013==0, feature_028==1, feature_018==1, and high feature_033 (especially top quartile, >=~20).

analyses.code: Two t-tests: feature_023 effect inside vs outside the final 4-predicate subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=4588, predicates: f013=1 & f028=0 & f018=0 & f033<median): feature_023=1 mean=8.946 (n=3001), =0 mean=5.674 (n=1587), diff=+3.272, p=2.22e-241. Outside (n=45412): diff=+0.052, p=1.49e-02. Subgroup-vs-rest contrast in treatment effect ~+3.27 mo. Final subgroup hypothesis: feature_023 prolongs pfs_months only in patients with feature_013=1, feature_028=0, feature_018=0, and feature_033 below the cohort median.
